title: ranunculus acris var. sylvaticus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. sylvaticus, also known as the Wood Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The Wood Buttercup is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and easing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be cautious due to its potential toxicity and skin irritation.
